key_points: Coordinates help in locating a point precisely on a plane
Signs of x and y coordinates determine the quadrant of a point
Points lying on axes have one coordinate equal to zero
Distance of a point from axes is measured using absolute values
examples: Point (−3, 4) lies in the second quadrant
Distance of (2, −6) from X-axis is 6 units
exam_tips: Check signs carefully before identifying quadrant
Use equal scale on both axes while plotting
Points on axes do not belong to any quadrant
